prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard) in the gas phase at charge 0, multiplicity 1. The geometry was read from the provided .xyz file without re-optimization. PySCF (DFT backend) was used. All SCF calculations converged successfully, and charge conservation checks for the f⁺ and f⁻ indices passed (both sum to 1.0000). Key findings: (1) The most electrophilic site is C-4 (ortho to NO₂, f⁺ = 0.4964), and (2) the most nucleophilic site is C-5 (para to NO₂, f⁻ = 0.1073). The oxygen atoms of the NO₂ group also show high electrophilic character (f⁺ ≈ 0.47 each). Per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptors are provided in the results table above. A reactivity visualization has been generated and saved. The dual descriptor clearly shows that the NO₂-bearing carbons are electrophilic (positive values), consistent with the electron-withdrawing nature of the substituent.</prose>
